note: Manish is a close friend from college and an incredibly sharp React developer. He has 3 years at Byju's building their interactive learning UI, handles TypeScript like a pro, and wrote one of the most elegant component library architectures I've seen. He's been looking for his next challenge and I genuinely believe he'd be a great addition to the team. Strongly recommend scheduling an interview ASAP — he has other offers in play.

Amrita was my colleague at my previous company (Razorpay) where she served as HRBP for the engineering division of ~150 people. She was exceptional at navigating complex employee relations situations and was deeply respected by both leadership and employees. She left Razorpay to care for a family member and is now ready to return full-time. Her people skills and startup savvy make her a natural fit for our HRBP role.

Rohit and I worked on the same backend squad at Zepto for two years. He built our order management microservice from scratch using FastAPI and PostgreSQL and it has been rock solid ever since. He's deeply comfortable with async Python, knows Redis and queuing systems well, and writes incredibly clean, well-tested code. He's been on my referral shortlist for a while — this role is perfect for him. I am very confident he will exceed expectations.

Neeraj is a former MBA Finance classmate from NMIMS Mumbai. He has been a finance analyst at Godrej Industries for the past 3 years handling MIS, budgeting, and variance analysis. He is methodical, highly dependable, and has strong Excel modeling skills. He is exploring a move to a tech company for better growth opportunities. I believe his analytical rigor and structured thinking would fit our finance function very well.

Lalita is a friend from my previous company in operations. She has been in account management for about 2 years and expressed interest in transitioning into customer success. I know she's a hard worker and is eager to grow, though I'll be honest that she is still building the technical depth expected for a CSM role here. I am referring her mainly to give her a chance — she may need a more junior-level opportunity to start with.
